hero.headline: Get Your IRDA License with Expert Guidance
hero.subtext: Become an authorized insurance intermediary. Trusted by professionals and startups alike.

mainHeading: IRDA License Registration Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: IRDA License is granted by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) to the entities or individuals that wish to act as insurance brokers, agents, corporate agents, web aggregators, and third-party administrators. It is an obligatory licence as per the provisions of Section 8 of the IRDAI Act, 1999 for carrying any insurance business in India. Professionals Law provides the entire help in obtaining the IRDA license and maintaining IRDAI compliance provisions and regulations.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: You will be needing to hold an IRDA License to operate legally in the insurance industry in India. Regardless of if they sell insurance coverage, provide advisory services, or administrate policies as intermediates, companies need to be in possession of a valid IRDAI license. Without it, any insurance business or duty is deemed to be unauthorized and be subject to penalties or punishment.

body.title: Legal Authorization to Sell Insurance
body.description: Holding an IRDA license provides official recognition and legal authority to distribute and manage insurance products.

body.title: Increased Business Credibility
body.description: A licensed entity is more trusted by customers, insurers and regulatory bodies, improving professional credibility.

body.title: Wider Market Access
body.description: License holders can offer services across various insurance segments—life, health, general and reinsurance—depending on the license type.

body.title: Revenue Opportunities
body.description: IRDA license opens multiple income streams through commissions, policy management and insurance consulting.

body.title: Association with Top Insurance Companies
body.description: Licensed brokers and agents are eligible to collaborate with major insurers for selling and servicing policies.

criteria: Must be a registered company or LLP (for corporate agents/brokers)
Individuals must meet qualification and training standards prescribed by IRDAI
Minimum capital requirements (varies by license type, e.g., ₹75 lakh for direct brokers, ₹5 crore for reinsurance brokers)
Key managerial persons should have relevant experience and a clean legal and financial record
Must have infrastructure and IT systems in place to manage insurance operations

documents: Net worth certificate and audited financial statements
Details of directors/partners and key management personnel
KYC documents of promoters and directors
Proof of educational qualifications and training completion (for individuals)

body.description: Professionals Law helps determine the appropriate IRDA license type based on your business model.
Assist with company incorporation and capital requirements as per IRDAI norms.
Complete collection and verification of documents as required by IRDAI.
Submission of application through the IRDAI BAP portal.
Clarification and inspection process by the authority.
Upon successful scrutiny, IRDAI issues the license with a unique registration number.

Operating without an IRDA license, misrepresentation of policies, non-compliance with renewal and reporting norms or engaging in unfair practices can attract hefty fines, license cancellation, suspension or legal action under the IRDAI Act and associated regulations.

The licensing process typically takes 90 to 120 days from the date of application submission, depending on the category of license and completeness of documentation. Professionals Law ensures thoroughness and timely follow-ups to avoid delays.

body.question: Who needs an IRDA license?
body.answer: Anyone involved in selling, advising or managing insurance policies—agents, brokers, TPAs, web aggregators—must obtain an IRDA license.

body.question: Can an individual apply for an IRDA license?
body.answer: Yes, individuals can apply to become insurance agents after meeting training and qualification requirements.

body.question: What is the validity of the IRDA license?
body.answer: Typically, licenses are valid for three years, subject to renewal by complying with conditions.

body.question: Can one entity hold multiple insurance licenses?
body.answer: Not usually. One must apply separately for each license type (e.g., broker, TPA) and adhere to IRDAI segregation norms.

body.question: What are the capital requirements for corporate brokers?
body.answer: Direct brokers must maintain at least ₹75 lakh, composite brokers ₹5 crore and reinsurance brokers ₹4 crore.

body.question: Is it mandatory to have a physical office?
body.answer: Yes, IRDAI mandates office space, infrastructure and trained personnel to ensure proper operations and record-keeping.

body.question: Can foreign investment be made in IRDA license holders?
body.answer: Yes, subject to 49% FDI cap for insurance intermediaries under the automatic route.

body.question: How can Professionals Law help?
body.answer: Professionals Law provides expert advisory, registration services, compliance support and end-to-end coordination with IRDAI officials.

body.question: What happens if a license is not renewed on time?
body.answer: Delayed renewals can result in lapse of license and discontinuation of insurance operations until fresh approval is obtained.
